what_to_do_results.task: Configure and Upload Overtime Policies for Payroll Calculation
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Settings > Attendance > Overtime Policies. Verified 5 policies exist. Opened policy editor showing 3-step wizard (Policy Name & Calculation, Assign Employees, Review & Save). Confirmed rate formula: ((multiplier X (Basic + Allowances)) / (Working Days X Hours)) X Extra hours. Verified Single Rate/Custom Rate options. Confirmed Payroll table has Overtime column. Found Mass Upload Variable Pay for bulk overtime entry.

what_to_do_results.task: Review and manage extra hours adjustments
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Time > Employee attendance > Time and Pay Adjustments. Used Extra Hours quick filter (3 pending). Clicked on employee row to see detail panel. Expanded Attendance Details showing check-in/out, schedule, extra hours. Switched to Approved tab showing 7 approved extra hours with Payroll and Leave compensation types.

what_to_do_results.task: Configure Days In Lieu policies for non-monetary overtime compensation
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Settings > Attendance > Days In Lieu Policies. Found 5 policies. Opened CS Overtime Policy editor showing 3-step wizard. Confirmed calculation basis: 'How many hours that make a day in lieu?' set to 8 hours. Employee extra hours request toggle available.
Navigated to Settings > Attendance > General section. Found 'Minimum extra hours threshold' field set to 30 Minutes. This controls the minimum amount of extra hours before they count toward overtime.

what_to_do_results.task: Enable line manager compensation type selection
what_to_do_results.result: Found 'Line managers can decide compensation types for extra hours worked' toggle in General settings, currently enabled. This allows line managers to choose between overtime pay or days in lieu for employee extra hours.

what_to_do_results.task: View overtime amounts in payroll table
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Payroll > Payroll table. Confirmed Overtime column visible with AED 0.00 total. Column checkbox 'Overtime (AED 0.00)' available in column selector. Individual employee rows show overtime amounts.

what_to_do_results.task: Mass upload overtime via Excel template
what_to_do_results.result: From Payroll table, clicked 3-dot menu > Mass Upload Variable Pays. Dialog shows Download Excel template and Upload Excel file buttons. Spreadsheet pre-populated with all employees. Pay Item Type column for selecting Overtime. Format: .xlsx.

what_to_do_results.task: Review activity and comments on extra hours records
what_to_do_results.result: Opened extra hours detail panel for John Jacob. Expanded Activity and Comments section showing audit trail of actions taken on the record.

Opened Overtime Pay policy editor. Formula displayed: ((Unit [1.5] X [Basic + Allowance]) / [Custom Days] [30.42] X [Hours: 8]) X Extra hours duration. Rate type: Single Rate with Unified/Split toggle. Allowances selector for which salary components to include.

what_to_do_results.task: Schedule Overtime in Advance for Employees
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Time > Employee attendance > Time and Pay Adjustments > Scheduled tab. Found 'Schedule Extra Hours' button in top-right. Clicked it to open the scheduling dialog. Dialog fields: Employee dropdown (searchable, shows 'Not assigned to addition policy' for ineligible employees), Date (YYYY-MM-DD with calendar picker), Extra Hours Duration (Hours + Minutes), Current Schedule section (shows shift info or 'No Published Shifts' alert), Compensation Type radio buttons (Approve for Payroll / Approve for Leave), Description (optional text field). Scheduled tab table columns: Name, Created At, Type, Scheduled For (date, time range, location), Unit(s), Compensation Type, Status. Quick filters: Scheduled (2), Declined (0), Not Fulfilled (1). 3 existing scheduled entries found with statuses Scheduled (Expired) and Not Fulfilled. Note: Form uses duration (hours/minutes) not start/end time as WTD-010 expected.

what_to_do_results.task: Bulk Approve or Reject Pending Overtime Requests
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Time > Employee attendance > Time and Pay Adjustments > Pending tab. Confirmed checkboxes present on every row. Filtered to Extra Hours (3 pending entries). Selected 2 entries (John Jacob, Admin Nadeem) - bulk action bar appeared showing '2 Items Selected' with buttons: Reject All (2), Approve all for payroll (1), Approve all for leaves (1). Then selected all 3 entries - bar updated to: Reject All (3), Approve all for payroll (1), Approve all for leaves (2). Bulk action counts are smart - they reflect how many selected items match each compensation type. Individual row actions also available: Reject, Approve for Payroll/Leaves, and a 3-dot menu. Header checkbox enables select-all.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-001: Overtime calculations may vary based on policy configuration
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Different overtime policies can produce different payment amounts for the same extra hours depending on rate type (Single vs Custom), multiplier, and salary components included
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Verified in overtime policy editor - Single Rate uses one multiplier for all hours, Custom Rate allows different multipliers for different hour ranges. Salary components (Basic only vs Basic + Allowances) directly affect the hourly rate calculation.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-002: No dedicated overtime mass upload option in payroll
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: There is no separate 'Mass Upload Overtime' option in the payroll 3-dot menu. Overtime must be uploaded through 'Mass Upload Variable Pays' which covers all variable pay types
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Payroll table 3-dot menu shows: Download Gratuity File, Close Payroll Cycle, Mass Upload Variable Pays, Mass Upload Additions, Mass Upload Deductions. No dedicated overtime upload.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-003: Minimum extra hours threshold applies globally
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: The minimum extra hours threshold (currently 30 minutes) is a global setting that applies to all employees regardless of their overtime policy. Cannot be set per-policy.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: General settings shows single 'Minimum extra hours threshold' field (30 Minutes) with no per-policy override option visible in the overtime policy editor.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-004: Line manager compensation type toggle affects all managers
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: The 'Line managers can decide compensation types for extra hours worked' toggle is a global on/off setting, not configurable per team or department
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: General settings shows single toggle for line manager compensation type decision. No department-level or team-level override available.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-005: Days in lieu hours-per-day is fixed per policy
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: The 'How many hours that make a day in lieu' setting is fixed per policy and cannot vary by employee or day type within the same policy
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Days In Lieu Policy editor shows single 'How many hours that make a day in lieu?' field (8 Hours for CS Overtime Policy). No conditional logic for weekdays vs weekends.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-006: Overtime policy and Days In Lieu policy are separate configurations
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Administrators must configure overtime policies and days in lieu policies separately. There is no unified policy editor for both compensation types.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Settings > Attendance shows Overtime Policies and Days In Lieu Policies as separate accordion sections with independent policy lists and editors.

summary: Overtime v3 validation completed with supplemental gap analysis. The overtime feature in Bayzat operates across two main areas: (1) Time > Employee attendance > Time and Pay Adjustments for viewing and managing extra hours records with Pending/Approved/Rejected workflow, and (2) Settings > Attendance for configuring Overtime Policies and Days In Lieu Policies. Key findings: overtime supports two compensation modes (Payroll monetary amounts and Leave days in lieu), policy configuration uses a 3-step wizard with detailed rate formulas, the minimum extra hours threshold is 30 minutes globally, and line managers can be enabled to choose compensation types. Payroll integration includes an Overtime column in the payroll table and mass upload capability via Variable Pays. All 11 WTD tasks passed (including 2 supplemental v2 gap tasks). Supplemental findings: Schedule Extra Hours dialog uses employee dropdown, date picker, duration (hours/minutes), compensation type radio buttons, and optional description. Bulk actions on Pending tab show smart counts per compensation type (Reject All, Approve all for payroll, Approve all for leaves). 6 WOF items confirmed as by-design limitations.
